key_insight: The paper introduces a variational joint formulation that connects beams with arbitrary cross-sections using moving-frame offset constraints and eliminates Lagrange multipliers via a discrete null space method.
engineering_relevance: It enables beam-to-beam coupling without explicit rotational interface DOFs, making the joint broadly compatible with different beam and solid finite element kinematics.
method: Define offset coordinates in a moving cross-section frame to constrain or release relative beam position
Approximate the Lagrange multiplier field to enforce only resultant force and moment transfer
Eliminate multipliers with a discrete null space method to reduce system size and improve conditioning
evidence_signal: The abstract reports application to both an extensible-director beam element and a brick element in nonlinear elastostatics, with several numerical examples.
watch_item: Because the multiplier field is only first-order approximated, the joint transferability is limited to resultant force and moment rather than full stress distribution.
paper_title: A variable-offset joint formulation for beams with arbitrary cross-sections using a null space method
